commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f321307baa383df0290edfdad09a2bac356d19e5)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the Codex app-server event projector to emit every non-commentary assistant message delta as a partial reply and updates projector tests for unphased and superseded assistant items.

PR surface: Source -2, Tests +23. Total +21 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main only streams Codex assistant deltas when phase is already final_answer, while upstream Codex delta notifications do not carry phase. I did not run a live Codex app-server turn in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs a contributor or maintainer code update plus real behavior proof; automation cannot supply the external contributor's live setup proof.

target.title: fix(codex): stream non-final-answer assistant deltas as partials
target.body:
Codex partial-reply streaming was gated on an assistant item phase of `final_answer`. Newer Codex app-servers (>= 0.139) stream `item/agentMessage/delta` events without tagging that phase mid-turn, so the gate never fired and TUI / WebChat surfaces only rendered the final assembled reply in one chunk while the Codex CLI streamed normally.

Stream every non-commentary assistant delta as a partial reply and let turn completion pick the final user-visible message (it already excludes commentary and superseded intermediate items from the persisted reply). Commentary items stay progress-only.

Sibling of #86050 (same symptom on the `claude-cli` runtime; candidate fix #86649).

## What Problem This Solves

Agents running on the Codex app-server runtime (the default for `gpt-5*` models when OpenAI access is via ChatGPT OAuth) produced **no live partial streaming** in the TUI (`openclaw chat`) and WebChat/Control UI. The full answer appeared in a single chunk at turn completion, even though the interactive Codex CLI streams the same turn token-by-token.

Root cause: `CodexAppServerEventProjector.handleAssistantDelta` only forwarded `onPartialReply` when the item phase was `final_answer` (`shouldStreamAssistantPartial`). The Codex app-server I tested (`codex-cli 0.139.0`; OpenClaw currently manages `0.134.0`, see `version.ts`) does not tag streaming `item/agentMessage/delta` notifications with that phase â€” the phase is only resolved at item/turn completion â€” so the gate was always false and partials were dropped.

## Why This Change Was Made

The gateway, WebChat, and TUI already consume assistant deltas correctly once the projector emits them (same conclusion as the `claude-cli` sibling #86050 / #86649). The fix is therefore at the projector: emit a partial reply for every **non-commentary** assistant delta, and rely on turn completion to select the final user-visible message. Commentary-phase items continue to route to progress (`emitCommentaryProgress`) only.

Tradeoff (for reviewers): previously, non-`final_answer` deltas were kept internal to avoid briefly showing intermediate "coordination" prose. With this change an intermediate non-commentary item can stream and then be superseded by the final reply (the gateway already reconciles partials via `replace`, and turn completion still excludes it from the persisted reply). This matches Codex CLI behavior and restores streaming across app-server versions rather than silently breaking on newer ones.

## User Impact

- Restores incremental answer streaming in TUI and WebChat for Codex-runtime agents (all `gpt-5*` / ChatGPT-OAuth users), so a long turn shows progress instead of looking hung.
- No change to the persisted/delivered final message; commentary still surfaces as progress only.

## Evidence

**Diagnosis on the affected install**
- `openclaw status` showed the affected session on the Codex runtime: `agent:main:main  gpt-5.5  Runtime: OpenAI Codex`.
- Installed binary: `codex-cli 0.139.0`; OpenClaw managed/expected `0.134.0` (`extensions/codex/src/app-server/version.ts`).
- A real turn captured via `codex exec --json` delivered the answer as a single item **with no `phase` field**, confirming the phase isn't available mid-stream:
  ```json
  {"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_0","type":"agent_message","text":"hello world"}}
  ```
- `codex app-server generate-json-schema` (0.139.0) confirms `final_answer`/`commentary` still exist in the protocol but are not emitted on streaming deltas in practice.

**Automated tests** â€” `pnpm vitest run extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts`
```
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  96 passed (96)
```
- Added regression test: **"streams assistant deltas when the app-server omits the item phase"** (covers the 0.139.0 case â€” unphased deltas must stream).
- Updated two tests that encoded the old `final_answer`-only behavior:
  - "projects assistant deltas and usage into embedded attempt results" â€” unphased deltas now stream as partials.
  - "keeps intermediate agentMessage items out of the final visible reply" â€” intermediate item now streams live but is still excluded from the persisted reply.
- Unchanged/passing: "streams commentary agent messages as keyed progress events" (commentary still progress-only, no partial), "streams final-answer assistant deltas into partial replies".

**Lint** â€” `npx oxlint extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts` â†’ clean.
target.author: agonza1
